zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Beauchamp於1415年9月16日出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾亨利 卡素時，她的父親Richard Beauchamp34歲，母親Isabel Le Despenser15歲。她於1424年10月18日之前與Edward Neville在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1448年6月18日在英國艾可查阿培爾享年32歲，埋葬在英國倫敦特福利亞斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Beauchamp于1415年9月16日出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾亨利 卡素时，她的父亲Richard Beauchamp34岁，母亲Isabel Le Despenser15岁。她于1424年10月18日之前与Edward Neville在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1448年6月18日在英国艾可查阿培爾享年32岁，埋葬在英国倫敦特福利亞斯。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Beauchamp was born on 16 September 1415, in Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard Beauchamp, was 34 and her mother, Isabel Le Despenser, was 15. She married Edward Neville before 18 October 1424, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 18 June 1448, in Chappel, Essex, England, at the age of 32, and was buried in Whitefriars, London, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Beauchamp est née le 16 septembre 1415, à Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Beauchamp, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Isabel Le Despenser, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Edward Neville avant 18 octobre 1424, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 18 juin 1448, à Chappel, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterrée à Whitefriars, Londres, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Beauchamp am 16. September 1415 in Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Beauchamp 34 und ihre Mutter Isabel Le Despenser 15. Sie hat vor 18. Oktober 1424 in England, United Kingdom Edward Neville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 18. Juni 1448 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Chappel, Essex, England und wurde in Whitefriars, London, England begraben.

Lei sposato Edward Neville prima del 18 ottobre 1424, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Beauchampが1415年9月16日にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアハンリー カストルで生まれた時，父Richard Beauchampは34歳，母Isabel Le Despenserは15歳であった。1424年10月18日以前にイギリスイギリスでEdward Nevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1448年6月18日にイギリスエセックスチャップエルで32歳で死去し，イギリスロンドンウィテフリアルズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Beauchamp(이)가 1415년 9월 16일 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 핸리 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Beauchamp(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Le Despenser(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1424년 10월 18일 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 Edward Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1448년 6월 18일 영국 에세스 차플에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 런던 휘테프리아르스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Beauchamp родилась 16 сентября 1415 г. в местности ханли кастл, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard Beauchamp было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel Le Despenser, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edward Neville перед 18 октябрь 1424 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 18 июня 1448 г. в местности чаппел, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 32 лет и была похоронена в местности уитефрярс, Лондон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Beauchamp nació el 16 de septiembre de 1415, en Hanley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard Beauchamp, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Isabel Le Despenser, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Edward Neville antes de 18 de octubre de 1424, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 18 de junio de 1448, en Chappel, Essex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 32 años, y fue sepultada en Whitefriars, London, Inglaterra.
